The applicant must be from the Scheduled Tribe community. 1. The applicant must be above 18 years. 1. The beneficiary must have his/her own bank account. 1. The applicant must have an Aadhaar Card/Voter ID/Ration Card/Passport as proof of identity.
application_process: > **Registration Process:** **Step 1:** The applicant may visit and apply online at the [I-Khedut Portal](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/). **Step 2:** Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. **Step 3:** Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'. **Step 4:** Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'. > **Login Process:** **Step 1:** The applicant may visit and apply online at the [I-Khedut Portal](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/). **Step 2:** Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’. **Step 3:** Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open. **Step 4:** Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application. **Step 5:** Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application. **Step 6:** Once the application is done, confirm it. **Step 7:** Take a printout of the confirmed application. > **Contact Us:** [Click here](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/Public/frm_Public_Contacts.aspx).

scheme_name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Fish Seed Rearing by Women - Gujarat
implementing_agency: Commissioner Of Fisheries
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The scheme “Fish Seed Rearing by Women” is a component under the “To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas” scheme, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to increase fish production through inland fisheries and provide employment opportunities, particularly to Scheduled Tribe women engaged in fish seed rearing. Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided for fish seed rearing activities to women involved in inland fisheries. > **Objectives:** - To increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities. - To boost fish farming or fishing activities in freshwater bodies. - Fishing in reservoirs, aquaculture in ponds, and catch from rivers increase fish production and boost inland fisheries. - The main objective is to provide employment opportunities to Scheduled Tribe fishermen in the state and increase fish production.
benefits: - The ₹90/- will be paid per 1,000 numbers, with a minimum 30% recovery for spawn to fry. - The rearing time for fish seed is 30 days. - Minimum 1 year required before reclaiming benefits under this component again.
eligibility: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat. 1. The applicant must be an Individual woman involved in inland fisheries activities. 1. The applicant must be from the Scheduled Tribe. 1. The applicant must be above 18 years. 1. The beneficiary fisherwoman must have her own bank account. 1. The applicant must have an Aadhar Card/Voter ID/Ration Card/Passport as proof of identity. > **Preference/Priority/Reservation:****** The scheme is specifically targeted at Scheduled Tribe women.

scheme_name: Assistance to OBM Boat Owner Fishermen on Purchase of Kerosene/Petrol
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The scheme "Assistance to OBM Boat Owner Fishermen on Purchase of Kerosene/Petrol" has been launched by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to fishermen owning small boats with Outboard Motors (OBM) by subsidizing the cost of kerosene or petrol. This initiative supports the fishing community and promotes fisheries in Gujarat.
benefits: **Assistance under the scheme:** **Name of Component****Standard of Assistance****Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits**Assistance on the purchase of Kerosene/Petrol Maximum assistance of ₹50/- per literBenefits are available as per the prescribed quota throughout the year.
eligibility: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat. 1. The applicant must be a fisherman. 1. The applicant must be a Kerosene Card holder. 1. The applicant must own a small boat operated by an Outboard Motor (OBM).
application_process:
**Step 1:** Fishermen must purchase kerosene or petrol from government-approved fuel stations Gujarat Fisheries Central Co-operative Association Limited (G.F.C.C.A. Ltd.).
**Step 2:** Collect original bills of purchase.
**Step 3:** Submit an application along with the required documents to the concerned district office.

scheme_name: Meghalaya Agriculture Warehouse Scheme
nodal_department: Planning, Investment Promotion & Sustainable Development Department
scheme_details: The **Meghalaya Agriculture Warehouse Scheme,** under the Chief Minister’s CM-ELEVATE Program by the Planning, Investment Promotion & Sustainable Development Department, Government of Meghalaya, focuses on enhancing agricultural infrastructure through the establishment of warehouses across the state. It aims to reduce post-harvest losses, stabilize market prices, and improve farmer incomes. The scheme offers easy credit access via partner banks and substantial government subsidies to ease entrepreneurs’ financial burden. Implemented by the Meghalaya Basin Management Agency (MBMA), it supports individuals and registered entities in creating weatherproof storage for agricultural produce, enabling value addition, market linkages, and long-term storage for the farming community.
benefits: 1. 5% of project cost to be contributed by the entrepreneur 75% of project cost provided upfront by Government of Meghalaya as margin money support to the bank remaining 20% via bank loan. 1. Direct credit linkage with partner banks. 1. Guidance and handholding in the application process through implementing agencies. 1. Support in project realization for the warehouse venture. 1. Training and capacity building for entrepreneurs to manage post-harvest storage effectively. 1. Technical support for maintenance and operations of warehouses.
eligibility: **For Individuals:** 1. The applicant should be between 18 and 58 years of age. 1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya. 1. The applicant should have ownership of the land where the warehouse is proposed or leased land for at least the next 10 years. 1. The applicant should not be a credit defaulter of any bank. **For Registered Entities:** 1. The applicant should be based in the village where the program is being availed. 1. The applicant should have an active bank account and submit the bank statement of the last six months. 1. The applicant should have been in operation for at least two years. 1. The applicant should possess land, either self-owned or leased, duly verified by the competent authority. 1. The applicant should not be a credit defaulter of any bank.
application_process: **Step 1:** Visit the [official website ](https://meghalayaone.gov.in/meghalaya-one/view-detail?U2FsdGVkX1%20sDpCRZV49xwqwX0uVdCwRGm6XgtOYOQW%20yY283fK%2FmbfcOzbdVJHaT2i2QNL5Tlk6GTpBcFnlaDIk%203np32OP4yu9ipbvZ1Zo3kap3sz1twRg3EXWCt8UR31DulG7dvYHQuWy%20gupHMGLCHZ88r8ikwwxkJv5nohTCXehHn3oPMmJUUk%209Jcs0ZQIAWoeEv4Rz%20%20OlLwCYOwDoTfAakMghNbkA7DE1T%20FRLROu7RMKdvgGLRIGXpU=)of the scheme and click on the “Login to Apply” button to start the application process. Carefully read all the instructions provided on the website before proceeding. **Step 2:** If you are a first-time user, click on “[Registration for New User](https://meghalayaone.gov.in/meghalaya-one/citizen-login?U2FsdGVkX1%2Fj0VpCC5AKaBYOcVsO207ea%20qs40JOarPgwkZtFDg03EVeDNMjhzMNgt66jk7HLKmzLFayU0wTM0EjpeKCRqApCovt2efa%2F0KbydwrFt8QNtiK1L9FEGK0rwHWPMZNxfJ2oG84okmekHM%20w2UWsypPf7HznEGzXJ9yzUevH2GxeNY6GrVN64Nc5vLWhdycsyVT5gxYjOQ7aTLfMjvUcaNNg56%20lFH6Z87ejSfF5hcowpsYT63w1IVyMGXXLjTCwVX1J7zigJST3KvW3SAkeGKZbQ4pl9jSHq16%2FowHqRFwaSZ2hZHgvcAel7SmNx0C4xFN5oap8U%20rLA=%3D)” and complete the registration process to create your account. **Step 3:** Select your Authentication ID from the available options, such as - PDS (Ration Card) - Election Photo ID Card (EPIC). **Step 4:** Enter your card number, select your name from the displayed list, and click on “Next” to continue. **Step 5:** Your EPIC ID will appear on the screen. If the details are correct, click on “Accept.” If the details are incorrect or rejected, manually enter your EPIC number and proceed. **Step 6:** Click on “Validate” to verify the entered EPIC number, and then click on “Next” to move to the application form. **Step 7:** Fill in all required details in the application form, upload the necessary documents, carefully review all the information, and click on “Submit.” After successful submission, you will receive a unique application number for future reference. **Sign In** **Step 1:** Click on [“Sign In](https://meghalayaone.gov.in/meghalaya-one/citizen-login?U2FsdGVkX1%2Fdu5Z2a1hGWoNI9swJd%2F0ipy%2F8BIg8oHzgeQ8x8B%206kEL5r6nzjssTJEkSC6a%2FSWz7TWB3omsCH8BvMqNoVkXYB3r12F2IIJu4JPz98fEyQ9bC3ERAjVoMX%2FaXvBWvVd34iUrwhVjn5iB2jCAqiKeCkjFBb9FIzX%203uUzYAhHc35dE4aSDFDctKc13r1MR%2FNuq4idI8ilaZQ5JUjUgutxygxHcnRmHRuYW0dbzfMIpBADd90WhKa76ESANqQ4hUqAzhMpEd1WWAxerZ55GBq1cWB8NmElKfsgqW%20Go8qlNIhySwptuQ1imQF4xKBa437k7OlxbylI9ig=%3D)” and choose your preferred sign-in method from Mobile Number, PDS, EPIC, or Member ID. **Step 2:** Enter your User ID and Password, then click on “Login” to access your account. **Step 3:** After logging in, click on “Apply for Services/Scheme” to start your application. **Step 4:** Fill in all the required details in the application form, upload the necessary documents, carefully review the information, and click on “Submit.” **Note:** **Online Submission:** Apply via the CM-ELEVATE Portal with all required documents as per the eligibility criteria **Screening:** Applications will be verified based on submitted documents by the Meghalaya Basin Management Agency (MBMA). Shortlisting will be done after document verification **Interview & Inspection:** Shortlisted applicants may undergo personal interviews conducted by the State Level Evaluation Committee and a physical inspection of the proposed land to verify ownership or lease **Final Approval:** The State Level Evaluation Committee, headed by the CEO/ED of MBMA with banking partners as members, will evaluate and recommend applications for sanction.

scheme_name: FOCUS Scheme (Farmers Collectivization For Upscaling Production and Marketing System)
scheme_details: The **FOCUS Scheme** (Farmer’s Collectivization for Upscaling Production and Marketing Systems) is one of the flagship schemes of the Planning Department, Government of Meghalaya. Under the scheme, an annual cash benefit ranging from ₹50,000 to ₹1,00,000 is provided to Agriculture and Agri-allied Producer Groups (PGs). Each PG may consist of 10 to 20 members. The benefit amount is credited directly to the PG’s bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).
benefits: 1. The subsidy is disbursed through approved financing partners. 1. Loans are provided by selected partner banks with a repayment period of up to 15 years. 1. There are no pre-closure penalties on the loan. 1. The Government of Meghalaya pays 80% of the total project cost upfront. 1. The remaining 20% is provided as a bank loan (5% paid by the entrepreneur and 75% by the government). 1. Agriculture and Agri-allied Producer Groups (PGs) receive an annual cash benefit ranging from ₹50,000 to ₹1,00,000.
eligibility: 1. The Producer Group must consist of 10 to 20 producers from the same village. 1. The members must be engaged in the same or similar agricultural or allied activities with high income-generation potential. 1. The group must formally form a Producer Group (PG) under the scheme. 1. Each member must hold a valid Family ID/Citizen ID of Meghalaya. 1. All members must belong to different households. 1. Each member must have a separate and unique Family ID of Meghalaya.

scheme_name: Construction of Water Storage Tanks for Drip Irrigation
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The scheme &quot;Construction of Water Storage Tanks for Drip Irrigation&quot; introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat,  aims to offer financial assistance to farmers for setting up cemented water tanks essential for efficient drip irrigation.  It supports sustainable agriculture through water conservation, especially for horticultural practices.
benefits: - **Unit cost of construction:** ₹1,00,000/- per tank.  - **For General category farmers:** 50% subsidy, up to ₹50,000/-.  - **For SC/ST farmers:** 75% subsidy, up to ₹75,000/-.
eligibility:
1. The applicant must be a farmer.
   1.  The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
   1. The applicant must plan to construct a cemented water storage tank of at least 25.50 cubic meters capacity.

> **Note:** Cemented mature water tanks, drip set mandatory.

**Step 1:** The applicant may visit and apply online at I-Khedut Portal: [https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/)
**Step 2:** On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.

**Step 4:** Click on &quot;Apply New&quot; button and submit a new application.

nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department

By encouraging the use of efficient and innovative agricultural inputs, the scheme strives to boost farm productivity, improve crop yields, and raise the overall economic well-being of farmers.
