scheme_name: Establishment of Poultry Valley
scheme_details: The scheme "Establishment of Poultry Valley" was introduced by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote sustainable and organized poultry farming among cooperative society members, especially women, by providing financial and technical support for poultry rearing, thereby enhancing income generation and livelihood opportunities in the state.
benefits: - Under the project, each farmer will rear 750 chicks annually in three phases. The Animal Husbandry Department will supply 500 one-day-old chicks from the state sector budget through M-PACS, while the farmer will bear the cost of the remaining 250 chicks. - The Cooperative Department will provide an interest-free loan for the construction of poultry sheds and for the care & rearing of the chicks.
eligibility: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand. 1. The applicant must be a member of a Multi-Purpose Agricultural Cooperative Society (MPACS). 1. Preference will be given to female beneficiaries under this scheme. 1. The applicant must not have any outstanding loans from MPACS or any other bank. 1. The selected applicant must be eligible to receive Medium-Term (MT) and Short-Term (ST) loans under the Deendayal Upadhyaya Krishi Kalyan Yojana. 1. The applicant must own or have land on lease.
application_process: Beneficiaries must apply through the Cooperative Department and the Veterinary Officer. The application must submit the following documents along with application form: Aadhaar card, bank account details, land records/registry/lease documents, and a certificate or affidavit from the concerned bank stating that the applicant is not a loan defaulter.

scheme_name: Gau Palan Yojana
scheme_details: The scheme "Gau Palan Yojana" was introduced by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote cow rearing among eligible Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries, enhancing their livelihood and income generation by providing financial support for cow rearing units.
benefits: Under this scheme, a milch cow up to the fourth stage of pregnancy is provided. Of the total cost of ₹40,000/-, 90% (₹36,000/-) is borne by the state government, while 10% (₹4,000/-) is to be contributed by the beneficiary.
eligibility: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand. 1. The applicant must belong to the General, Scheduled Caste, or Scheduled Tribe category. 1. The applicant must be included in the SECC (Socio-Economic and Caste Census) category. 1. The applicant should have an interest in cow rearing.
application_process: - Interested beneficiaries must submit their proposal in an open meeting of the Gram Sabha. Once the proposal is approved, the beneficiary shall submit it to the Veterinary Officer, Livestock Extension Officer, or Gram Panchayat Development Officer of the respective area, or the Gram Panchayat may submit it on behalf of the beneficiary. - Based on the recommendation of the village-level selection committee, the final selection is made by the development block-level committee and forwarded to the Animal Husbandry Department. After selection, the beneficiary will receive funds for purchasing the cow through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) within one to two months. - Applicants must submit Aadhaar card, bank account details, and caste certificate along with their proposal.

scheme_name: Atma Nirbhar Plantation Yojana
scheme_details: The scheme &quot;Atma Nirbhar Plantation Yojana (ANPY)&quot; was launched by the Department of Agriculture, Government of Arunachal Pradesh, aims to promote self-reliance in the agriculture sector through sustainable plantation activities across the state&#39;s diverse agro-climatic zones. Under this scheme, support in the form of plantation assistance and allied resources is provided to individual farmers, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), and Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
benefits: - Access to plantation support including technical guidance, planting material, and financial assistance.
eligibility:
- The Scheme can be applied by individual farmers as well as Self-Help Groups (SHGs)/ Farmers Producer Organizations (FPOs).

**Eligibility Criteria for Individual Farmers**

1. The applicant should be a native of Arunachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be an individual farmer. 
1. The applicant should have landholding between 0.50 hectares and 25 hectares.
1. The applicant should not be a bank defaulter.
1. The applicant should not be a government servant.

**Eligibility Criteria for Self-Help Groups (SHGs)**

1. The applicant should be a Self-Help Group (SHG) based in Arunachal Pradesh. 
1. The SHG should have a minimum of 1.0 hectare and up to 50 hectares of land.
1. The SHG should not be a bank defaulter.
1. An already existing SHGs prior to involvement of SrLM shall be considered.
1. SHGs formed for small-scale industries such as weaving, knitting, tailoring, and other similar businesses shall not be entertained or considered eligible.

**Note:** The SHGs formed especially for Agri- sector shall be given priority.
**Eligibility Criteria for Farmers Producer Organizations (FPOs)**

1. The Farmer Producer Organization (FPO) should be registered in Arunachal Pradesh. 
1. The FPO should have a minimum of 5 hectares and a maximum of 50 hectares of land.
1. The FPO should have at least 100 shareholders.
1. The FPO should submit at least one audited Balance Sheet.
1. All board members and shareholders of FPO should be non-defaulters to any bank/financial institution.
1. All types of FPOs engaged in agriculture business will get benefit from this programme.
application_process:
***Interested and eligible students need to apply online on the*** [***Atma Nirbhar Yojana***](https://agri.arunachal.gov.in/atmanirbhar_yojana/) ***portal.*** 
**New User Registration**

   1. Select the &quot;[Login](https://agri.arunachal.gov.in/atmanirbhar_yojana/login)&quot; option.   1. Subsequently, click on the &quot;[Register Here&quot;](https://agri.arunachal.gov.in/atmanirbhar_yojana/user-register) link.   1. Complete the registration form with the requisite details, including your name, Aadhaar card number, email ID, and mobile number.
   1. Establish a password for your account.
   1. Upon successful registration, the applicant will receive a User ID and password.

***Login &amp; Application Process:***

   1. [Log in](https://agri.arunachal.gov.in/atmanirbhar_yojana/login) using the provided User ID and password to access the application form.   1. Fill in the complete application form.
   1. Upload the required documents, including a photograph, caste certificate, residential certificate, and land ownership proof, etc.
   1. Submit the application form. 



**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the District Agriculture Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**OR**
The interested applicant downloads the [application form](https://cdn.s3waas.gov.in/s3c0c7c76d30bd3dcaefc96f40275bdc0a/uploads/2023/05/2023051921.pdf) from the official website. 
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District Agriculture Office.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Accident Insurance Scheme - Uttarakhand
scheme_details: The scheme "Accident Insurance Scheme", a sub-component of the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana, is implemented by the Department of Fisheries, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide financial security to fish farmers in the event of accidental death, permanent disability, or hospitalization.
benefits: **Under this scheme, an annual insurance coverage is provided as follows:** - ₹5,00,000/- in case of accidental death or total permanent disability. - ₹2,50,000/- for partial disability (such as loss of limbs, fingers, or partial loss of vision/hearing/speech, etc.). - ₹50,000/- for hospitalization expenses due to accidents. - The fish farmer is not required to pay any insurance premium. The entire premium amount is borne by the Department.
eligibility: 1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand. 1. The applicant must be engaged in fish production or fishing as a profession. 1. The applicant may belong to any caste category, including General, Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Other Backward Class (OBC), or be a woman. 1. The applicant’s age must be between 18 and 70 years at the time of enrollment.
application_process: - To avail of the benefits under the scheme, eligible beneficiaries can obtain the insurance application form through the field personnel of the Fisheries Department. - The duly filled application form must be submitted along with the fisherman’s Aadhaar card, bank account details, birth certificate, permanent residence certificate, nominee details, and Aadhaar card. The insurance premium is paid by the department. After the fisherman is insured, the department will issue the relevant insurance certificate. - In the event of an accident, death, or hospitalization, the beneficiary’s family must immediately inform the field personnel/officers of the Fisheries Department in the concerned area. - In case of an accident, the claim form along with the necessary documents, such as a medical certificate, death certificate, hospitalization certificate, and insurance certificate, must be submitted. The claim amount will then be disbursed accordingly.

scheme_name: Krishak Utpadak Kshati Sahayata Yojana
implementing_agency: Uttarakhand Agricultural Production & Marketing Board
scheme_details: The scheme “Krishak Utpadak Kshati Sahayata Yojana” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Agricultural Production & Marketing Board under the Department of Agriculture, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance and compensation to farmers in the event of loss or damage to crops, livestock, or agricultural land caused by fire, flood, or heavy rains in the notified areas of the state.
benefits: Damage caused by fire accidents to standing crops, harvested crops in the field, crops stored in barns for threshing, or residual produce in the notified market areas of Uttarakhand State, as well as damage to property and livestock due to fire in the farmer’s house, and damage to agricultural land due to floods or rain-induced soil erosion, all such losses make farmers eligible to receive compensation under this scheme.
eligibility: 1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand. 1. The applicant must be a minimum of 18 years of age to submit and receive a claim.
application_process: - For reporting fire or flood accidents, the affected farmer/producer must submit an application in the prescribed format (available free of cost at the Mandi Samiti office) to the Secretary, Chairman, Administrator, or Tehsildar of the concerned Mandi Samiti area within seven days of the incident. - The received claim will be investigated by the Secretary of the Market Committee, and the inquiry must be completed within one week. The final report shall be submitted to the Chairman/Administrator of the Mandi Committee in the prescribed format attached to the claim form. In areas where the Mandi Committee does not have an office, the concerned Tehsildar will conduct the inquiry and forward it to the respective Mandi Committee. - After thorough scrutiny of the claim form by the Mandi Samiti office, the payment process will be initiated with the approval of the Chairman/Administrator of the Mandi Samiti. - All the above proceedings should be completed within two months, and in special circumstances, the Chairman/Administrator of the Mandi Committee may extend the time period as required. - For the payment of assistance claims, the Mandi Board will prepare a bank draft of the sanctioned amount in the name of the claimant and send it to the Secretary of the concerned Mandi Committee for disbursement.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Ice Plant/Cold Store of Minimum 30-Ton Capacity - Haryana
scheme_details: The Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Ice Plant/Cold Store of minimum 30-ton capacity, introduced by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to provide the subsidy to support the establishment of ice plants or cold storage facilities with a minimum capacity of 30 tons to strengthen fish preservation and cold chain infrastructure in Haryana. This centrally sponsored scheme is jointly funded 50% by the Central Government and 50% by the State Government. Beneficiaries must submit an undertaking in their DPR/SCP to keep the unit operational, bear all post-construction costs, and ensure ice is supplied to fishers and fish farmers at affordable prices.
benefits:
- The project cost is ₹120,00,000/-

> **Subsidy Distribution :**


1. A 40% subsidy is available for the General Category.
1. A 60% subsidy is offered for SC (Scheduled Caste) and Women beneficiaries.
eligibility:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Haryana and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant must submit an undertaking in the DPR/SCP to bear all operational, maintenance, and post-construction management costs, and to ensure the continuous operation of the ice plant/cold storage.
1. The applicant must note that central assistance will be provided based on the actual cost, within the overall ceiling specified under the relevant PMMSY sub-components.
1. The applicant must ensure that the ice produced is supplied to fishers and fish farmers at an affordable price.
1. The applicant must either own the project land or hold it on a long-term registered lease of at least 10 years to be eligible.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Construction of New ponds for Saline /Alkaline areas-Haryana
scheme_details: The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana: Construction of New Ponds for Saline/Alkaline Areas" by the Department of Fisheries, Government of Haryana, aims to expand fisheries in various urban and semi-urban areas. Through this scheme, subsidies for the construction of new ponds and central financial assistance for the initial fish crop on newly constructed ponds are provided to beneficiaries. The scheme considers ponds with a long duration and a registered lease period of at least 7 years for grants.
benefits: - The project cost is ₹8,00,000/- per hectare. - The subsidy is limited to 40% of the project cost for the General Category and 60% of the project cost for Scheduled Caste (SC) and women beneficiaries.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The construction of new ponds subsidy will be provided by the state government for the expansion of fisheries in various urban/semi-urban areas only under the construction of new ponds under the Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) scheme.
1. Only central financial assistance will be provided to the beneficiaries for the initial fish crop only on the fertiliser of newly constructed ponds.
1. Central financial assistance on the construction of new ponds will be provided only on ponds prepared for fisheries.
1. Under this scheme, ponds whose pond duration will be for a long time, and the registered lease period will be at least 7 years, will be considered for a grant on those ponds.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Construction of Fish Kiosks Including Kiosks of Aquarium/Ornamental Fish-Haryana
scheme_details: The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana: Construction of Fish Kiosks including Kiosks of Aquarium/Ornamental fish" by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to provide central assistance for the construction of fish kiosks, including those for aquarium/ornamental fish.
benefits: The project cost is ₹10,00,000/- per unit. Subsidy of 40% of the project cost for General Category beneficiaries. Subsidy of 60% of the project cost for SC/Women beneficiaries.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant should submit an undertaking in the Detailed Project Report (DPR)/Self Contained Proposal (SCP) to the effect that all operational, maintenance, and post-construction management costs of the infrastructure facilities shall be borne by them.
1. The applicant should ensure the fish kiosk will be kept in operational condition for 10 years.
1. The applicant should display a board permanently at the infrastructure facilities to the effect that the fish kiosk is constructed with Central Assistance under the Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY).
1. The applicant can only avail of central assistance for one unit.
1. The projects having land on long-term registered lease shall also be considered for Central financial assistance under the scheme. However, the lease period/ agreement shall not be less than a minimum period of 10 (ten) years.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Inputs for fresh water Aquaculture including Composite fish culture, Scampi, Pangasius, Tilapia etc - Haryana
scheme_details: The Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Inputs for fresh water Aquaculture, including Composite fish culture, Scampi, Pangasius, Tilapia, etc, introduced by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to expand fisheries production in Haryana. This centrally sponsored scheme is jointly funded 50% by the Central Government and 50% by the State Government. It provides input cost subsidies for the first crop in newly constructed ponds under PMMSY, aiming to promote aquaculture in urban and semi-urban areas.
benefits:
- The project cost is: 4,00,000/- per hectare

> **Subsidy Distribution:**


1. 40% of the project cost for General Category beneficiaries
1. 60% of the project cost for SC/Women beneficiaries
eligibility:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Haryana with a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The applicant will be eligible for input cost subsidy provided by the State Government only for the expansion of fisheries in urban or semi-urban areas under the construction of new ponds.
1. The applicant will receive central financial assistance only for the initial fish crop (fertilizer) in newly constructed ponds.
1. The applicant will be eligible for central financial assistance on input costs only if the pond is properly prepared for fisheries.
1. The applicant may use land on a long-term registered lease, provided the lease period is at least 7 years, for eligibility under this scheme.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Construction of New Grow Out Ponds - Haryana
scheme_details: The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana: Construction of New Grow out Ponds" by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to expand fisheries in various urban and semi-urban areas through the construction of new grow-out ponds. Through this scheme, subsidies are provided for the construction of new grow-out ponds to beneficiaries who are engaged in fisheries.
benefits: - The scheme provides financial assistance for the Construction of the New Grow-out Ponds. The project cost is ₹7,00,000/- per Hectare. - The subsidy will be limited to 40% of the project cost for beneficiaries falling under the General Category. - The subsidy will be limited to 60% of the project cost for beneficiaries who are Scheduled Caste (SC) or Women.
eligibility:
1. The applicant must have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
1. The construction of new grow-out ponds subsidy will be provided by the State Government for the expansion of fisheries in various urban/semi-urban areas only under the construction of new grow-out ponds under the Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) scheme.
1. Central financial assistance for the construction of new grow-out ponds will be provided only for the ponds prepared for fisheries.
1. The applicant should have own land with clear title or having land on a long-term registered lease, not less than a minimum of 7 years.

scheme_name: Scheme for the Welfare of Schedule Caste Families in Fisheries Sector: Subsidy on 2nd Year to 5th Year Lease Money
scheme_details: The “Subsidy on 2nd Year to 5th Year Lease Money” is a scheme component under the Scheme for the Welfare of Scheduled Caste Families in the Fisheries Sector, launched by the Fisheries Department, Government of Haryana. The objective of the scheme is to provide full-time employment to individuals from Scheduled Caste families in the fisheries sector and uplift their economic status by promoting fish farming through financial assistance for leasing Panchayati ponds or watershed areas. Under this scheme, a subsidy is provided to Scheduled Caste beneficiaries to partially cover the lease amount for Panchayati ponds or watershed areas from the second year to the fifth year, thereby reducing long-term operational costs and facilitating the continuation of pond lease arrangements.
benefits: **Financial Assistance:** - 40% of the actual lease amount OR ₹40,000/- per hectare, whichever is less. - The subsidy is applicable for a maximum of 4 hectares per beneficiary. **Note:** Subsidy will be provided on lease money from 2nd to 5th year of lease.
eligibility: 1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana. 1. The applicant should have a valid Family ID Card (Parivar Pehchan Patra). 1. The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Caste category. 1. The applicant's age should be 18 years or above. 1. Subsidy will be provided on lease money from 2nd to 5th year of lease. 1. The maximum area eligible for subsidy is 4 hectares per applicant. 1. The subsidy is applicable only on Panchayati ponds and government water sheds.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Small Biofloc Establishment Gujarat
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
tags: Fish Farming|Biofloc|Recirculating Aquaculture System|Fish|Small Biofloc Establishment
scheme_details:
The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)" with a component of Small Biofloc Establishment (7 tanks 4mm diameter and 1.5m height) introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities. Through this scheme, inland fish farming is promoted using advanced technologies such as Biofloc and Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) to maximise fish production in less land and water. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
> **Objective:** - Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. - Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land. - Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
Small Biofloc Establishment (7 tanks 4mm diameter and 1.5m height): > **Financial Assistance:** - The unit cost is ₹7,50,000/- per unit. - General Category: 40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less. - Women/Scheduled Caste (S.C.) and Scheduled Tribe (S.T.) Categories: 60% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
> **Mode of Disbursement:** Assistance is paid through 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from State Governments. > **Validity:** The benefit for small biofloc establishment can be reclaimed once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
1. The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities. 1. The applicant must be above 18 years of age. 1. The applicant must have their own bank account. 1. The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted. 1. The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
> **Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:** - All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes. - Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.
application_process:

On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’. **Step 3:** Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open. **Step 4:** Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application. **Step 5:** Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application. **Step 6:** Once the application is done, confirm it. **Step 7:** Take a printout of the confirmed application.

scheme_name: National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The scheme "National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development" is a centrally sponsored scheme that aims to promote location-specific sustainable agriculture production by encouraging improved agronomic practices, soil health management, better rainwater utilization, judicious use of agrochemicals, crop diversification, and the progressive adoption of integrated farming systems involving crops, livestock, and trees.
benefits: - **Financial Help:** Provides financial assistance to eligible farmers to adopt sustainable agricultural practices. - **Improved Agronomic Practices:** Encourages the implementation of location-specific, modern, and efficient farming techniques. - **Soil Health Management:** Supports initiatives for maintaining and enhancing soil fertility and health. - **Enhanced Rainwater Use Efficiency:** Promotes techniques and infrastructure for optimal utilization of rainwater in agriculture. - **Judicious Use of Chemicals:** Advocates for reduced and responsible application of chemical fertilizers and pesticides. - **Crop Diversification:** Encourages farmers to cultivate a variety of crops, promoting resilience and better income opportunities. - **Integrated Farming Systems:** Supports the adoption of integrated crop-livestock-tree farming for holistic farm management.
eligibility: - The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat. - The applicant should be a farmer.

scheme_name: Scheme for Fruit and Vegetable Preservation and Training Program in Horticulture Department HRT 5
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The "Scheme for Fruit and Vegetable Preservation and Training Program in Horticulture Department HRT 5" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to empower women and urban citizens by offering hands-on training in value addition, preservation, canning, and kitchen-gardening of fruits and vegetables, along with promoting self-sustenance and nutritional well-being. scheme_name: Assistance for Protected Cultivation
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The scheme “Assistance for Protected Cultivation” by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat aims to promote modern, climate-resilient horticulture practices by providing financial support for protected cultivation infrastructure like mulching, nursery development, and plug nurseries. The scheme encourages self-employment and sustainable income generation for farmers through the use of polyhouses, shed net houses, and starter kits to improve crop productivity and resource efficiency.
benefits: > **Plastic Cover (Mulching):** - **General Area:** ₹32,000/- per hectare. - **Hilly Area:** ₹36,800/- per hectare. - 50% of the expenditure incurred, limited to 1 hectare per beneficiary. - **Self-Employment Oriented Horticulture Nursery Development Programme:** - **Nursery Structure:** ₹3,50,000/- per unit - ₹3,00,000/- per unit for 500 sq.m. area - **Starter Kit:** ₹50,000/- per unit - Assistance up to 65% of the cost or ₹2,27,500/-, whichever is less. > **Shed Net House & Polyhouse:** - **Coverage:** 500 sq.m to 4,000 sq.m - Assistance as per square meter cost based on empanelled company prices - Structures supported: Cylindrical, bamboo, and wooden. > **Plug Nursery:** - **Public sector:** ₹30,00,000/- per hectare for 1 hectare - **Private sector:** Assistance of 50% or ₹15,00,000/- per hectare (whichever is less) for 1 hectare.
eligibility: 1. The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat. 1. The applicant should be a farmer.

scheme_name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Agricultural Fair/Exhibition/Farmer Camp Gujarat
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The scheme "Distribution of Seeds of more productive varieties / Hybrid varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area (TASP)" with a component "Agricultural Fair/Exhibition/Farmer Camp" is introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide subsidized agricultural inputs to enhance productivity and livelihood of tribal farmers. Farmers belonging to scheduled tribes and tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas can achieve higher production of agricultural crops and improve their livelihood by using plant protection equipment, tarpaulins, field demonstrations, micronutrients, agriculture fairs, water carrying pipelines, castor cake/neem cake/LFOM/green manure, pump sets, 100% water-soluble fertilizer, plant protection pesticides, etc. These tools and practices, when used according to scientific recommendations, can significantly increase crop production and, consequently, improve their livelihood.
benefits:
The assistance is provided with a limit of ₹1,00,000/- can be spent for holding Agricultural Fair/Exhibition at the Taluka level. Farmer Shibir for 100 farmers, ₹15000/- can be spent within the limit.
> **Remarks:** Literature, tea, refreshments, meals, traveling expenses, exhibition stall fee, mandap, banner, photographs, sound system, electricity, furniture rent, Vs. Can be spent on things.
eligibility: 1. The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe. 1. The applicant should be a farmer. 1. The applicant should be located in tribal areas OR be a tribal cultivator residing outside tribal areas. 1. The scheme excludes farmers from Dang district.
application_process: Approach the Gram Sevak in your village for access to the Agricultural Fair/Exhibition/Farmer Camp under the scheme.

nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details: The scheme component "Replanting of Old Oil Palm Garden" launched under the "National Mission on Edible Oil–Oil Palm" scheme by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation, Government of Gujarat, aims to enhance oil palm production and lower dependency on imports by supporting farmers in uprooting and replanting old oil palm gardens that have reached the age of 25–30 years. The scheme provides a subsidy of ₹250 per plant for uprooting, replacing, and digging new pits, thereby ensuring sustainable cultivation practices and long-term productivity.

scheme_name: Rotavator/Thresher Machine Equipment Assistance Scheme
scheme_details: The “Rotavator/Thresher Machine Equipment Assistance Scheme” was launched by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to strengthen agricultural mechanization and improve the productivity of Scheduled Tribe farmers in tribal areas. Under this scheme, tribal farmers are provided with financial assistance to purchase essential farm machinery such as a 6-feet rotavator or a thresher machine, which they cannot afford due to poor economic conditions.
benefits: - Financial assistance to farmers to purchase a rotavator (6 feet) or a thresher machine.
eligibility: 1. The applicant should be a farmer. 1. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribes (ST) category. 1. The applicant should belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family. 1. The applicant should be a resident of tribal or forest areas of Gujarat. 1. The applicant should be a farmer dependent on agriculture as the main source of livelihood. 1. The applicant should intend to use the machinery for self-employment and agricultural purposes.
application_process: > **Application Process:** **Step 1:** Visit the [official website of](https://dsagsahay.gujarat.gov.in/)[ ](https://dsagsahay.gujarat.gov.in/)[Development Support Agency of Gujarat (D-SAG)](https://dsagsahay.gujarat.gov.in/), [Tribal Development Department](https://dsagsahay.gujarat.gov.in/). **Step 2:** Click on Beneficiary Registration. A [new page](https://dsagsahay.gujarat.gov.in/CitizenPortal/) will appears. Select "New beneficiary registration" if you are registering for the first time. **Step 3:** Register by entering your Ration Card Number and Mobile Number, then click GET OTP. **Step 4:** Enter the One Time Password (OTP) received on your mobile number and click Register. **Step 5:** Upon successful registration, a Success pop-up will appear. **Step 6:** Select your Applicant Type from the available options: Livestock Keeper, Farmer and Livestock Keeper, or Student/Trainee. Click Save. **Step 7:** Fill in the General Details, Personal Details, Bank Details, as required, and click the Proceed button. Confirm by clicking OK on the pop-up. **Step 8:** Upload the required documents by clicking Choose File and then Upload File. **Step 9:** After uploading all documents, a window will appear. Click View to check each document. If any document was uploaded incorrectly, click Delete and re-upload it. **Step 10:** Once all documents are verified, click the Proceed button. Confirm by clicking OK on the pop-up. **Step 11:** After completing these steps, your profile will be created, and the portal dashboard will be displayed. > **Tracking Process:** **Step 1:** Visit the [official website](https://dsagsahay.gujarat.gov.in/#) of Development Support Agency of Gujarat (D-SAG), Tribal Development Department. **Step 2:** Click on "Application Status". A pop-up window will appear. **Step 3:** Select the "Scheme name" from the options provided. **Step 4:** Enter your application number in the designated field. **Step 5:** Click on "Application Status" again to view the status of your application.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Refrigerated vehicles - Gujarat
implementing_agency: Commissioner Of Fisheries
nodal_department: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department
scheme_details:
The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Refrigerated vehicles", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.
> **Objective of the scheme** - Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So cold storage/ice plant modernization is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition. - Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers. - Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits:
1. The unit cost of a refrigerated vehicle is ₹25,00,000/-. 1. 40% assistance (up to ₹10,00,000/-) is provided for general category beneficiaries. 1. 60% assistance (up to ₹15,00,000/-) is provided for women and SC/ST category beneficiaries.
> **Conditions:** - The benefit will be available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India. - The benefit is available once in a lifetime.
> **Mode of Disbursement:** Contribution is shared: 60% by the Government of India and 40% by State Governments. > **Frequency of Disbursement:** One-time assistance. > **Validity:** Once in a lifetime for eligible beneficiaries.
eligibility: 1. The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state. 1. The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software. 1. The applicant should hold a fishing license. 1. The applicant should complete the online registration on the [iKhedut Portal](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/site/).1. The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan. 1. The applicant should have his/her own bank account. > **Preference/Reservation/Relaxation:** - Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost. - General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.
